data.description:
## Idea (parking lot — out of scope for the code-health analyzer epic 839b-f6d2)
Add a polyglot **dependency-graph** capability to the code-health analyzer (module/file coupling, fan-in/fan-out, cycles) so a per-project graph signal can feed the metrics registry.

## Why it's a separate idea, not part of the epic
The portability audit found there is **no mature, single, drop-in polyglot dependency-graph OSS CLI** (unlike LOC/complexity/duplication, which scc/lizard/jscpd cover). Realistic paths, all heavier:
- Per-language adapters behind the analyzer seam: dependency-cruiser / madge (JS/TS), pydeps (Python), `go mod graph` (Go), jdeps (Java).
- Polyglot foundations to build on (frameworks, not turnkey): tree-sitter + GitHub stack-graphs, or Sourcegraph SCIP indexers; heavyweight end: CodeQL.

## Open questions (to design before promoting out of idea)
- Which graph signals are worth a metric (cycle count? max coupling? fan-in hotspots?) and are they cross-language comparable?
- Single polyglot foundation (tree-sitter/SCIP) vs. a registry of per-language adapters with honest Unavailable where none exists?
- Cost/determinism/CI-time budget vs. value.

Treat as future work; do not block the scc/lizard/jscpd epic on it.
data.title: Polyglot dependency-graph analyzer capability for code-health metrics
